name: Product Grid
description: Purchasable items/posts grid with filtering, sorting, and multiple view modes. Used for creator content libraries.

standard.description: Card grid with filters and sorting

list.description: Stacked horizontal list view with product rows for a single-column browsing experience

visual_brief: Grid of product cards with a toolbar header containing filter dropdowns (category, price range, type), a sort selector (newest, popular, price), and view mode toggles (grid/list). Cards are arranged in a responsive CSS grid. The list preset stacks items as horizontal rows. The masonry preset uses a masonry layout for varied card heights. A 'Load More' button or infinite scroll pagination sits at the bottom. Empty state shows a 'No products found' message when filters return nothing.

responsive.mobile: Single-column card list. Filters collapse into a filter drawer button. Sort is a simple dropdown. Load more as a button.
responsive.tablet: Two-column grid. Filters visible in a row. Sort inline.
responsive.desktop: Three to four column grid. All filter and sort controls visible in the toolbar. Masonry layout effective at this width.

layout_hints.catalog_priority: Lead with discovery controls first, then the products themselves. The toolbar should support browsing without visually overpowering the merchandise.
layout_hints.view_consistency: Grid, list, and masonry variants should preserve the same product identity and pricing hierarchy so mode switching changes density, not meaning.
layout_hints.empty_state_role: No-results states should feel like browsing guidance, not a dead end. Keep them in the same rhythm as the catalog rather than replacing the whole surface with a different layout.

motion.micro: Cards, filters, and load-more affordances may sharpen or lift subtly on hover and focus.
motion.transitions: Sorting, filtering, and view changes should preserve catalog continuity and avoid noisy reshuffles that break scanability.

keyboard: Toolbar controls should precede products, and each product should expose one clear primary interaction target.
announcements: Product identity, pricing, availability, and active filters should be exposed semantically so the catalog remains understandable beyond grid layout alone.
